message:
feat(spec-explorer,components,e2e): deep-linkable URL state, mobile header, and comprehensive test coverage

Spec Explorer overhaul:
- Add deep-linkable URL state management so spec sections and search
  queries are preserved in the URL hash, enabling shareable links and
  browser back/forward navigation through spec exploration history
- Refactor spec-viewer-loader with progressive loading states and
  error boundaries for resilient content display
- Major spec-viewer rewrite: replace DOMPurify-based HTML rendering
  with a React AST-based markdown renderer that eliminates hydration
  mismatches, properly sanitizes links, and preserves modified-click
  behavior (cmd/ctrl+click opens in new tab)
- Add spec-docs asset bundle with structured Agent Mail domain
  documentation replacing placeholder registry content

Component improvements:
- Redesign site-header with responsive mobile navigation, animated
  hamburger menu, and scroll-aware background blur transitions
- Fix hydration mismatches in decoding-text and glitch-text by
  ensuring client-only animation state initialization
- Simplify scroll-to-top with IntersectionObserver-based visibility
- Clean up bottom-sheet and site-footer minor styling inconsistencies
- Remove unused client-shell initialization code

State management:
- Refactor site-state.tsx to use URL-synchronized state atoms with
  debounced hash updates, replacing the previous in-memory-only
  approach that lost context on page reload

Testing:
- Expand E2E smoke tests with mobile viewport regression coverage
- Refactor visualization E2E tests for deterministic assertions
- Add shared test fixtures for consistent viewport configurations
- Update playwright config with retry and timeout tuning
- Add content unit tests for spec document structure validation

Update beads issue tracking and Next.js type declarations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
